creator: joe@zircon.seattle.wa.us
text:
You claim that you know about problems with firefox3.  Yet, there is no 
information anywhere on the FreeBSD website which describes the pitfals 
and solutions required to use the product.

Therefore, I claim that since you do not provide documentation, the 
product is BROKEN and needs to be marked as BROKEN to prevent people 
from inadvertently doing the wrong thing.

If you cannot have firefox2 and firefox3 installed on the same system, 
please be EXPLICIT about it.  If you need to perform special actions in 
order to enable proper functioning of plugins in firefox3, please be 
EXPLICIT about it.  All you really need to do is put a link in UPDATING 
to let people know where to find the actual information.  There is 
NOTHING in the release notes about anything that you need to do to make 
plugins work.  Where is this information kept?  Why doesn't UPDATING 
have an explicit reference?  Why is there no information at all about 
known problems anywhere in the documentation?

?Joe
